events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes transformers (dry-type & pad-mount) work that is "in-progress" but shows work after a hold point without the prerequisite step shown and no signoff page attached. This is a sequence violation - work proceeded past a hold point without documented signoff of the prerequisite step.

Key issues:
1. Work is past a hold point but prerequisite step not shown
2. No signoff page attached
3. This is a sequence violation - hold points require signoff before proceeding

Decision: fail (non-compliant - proceeded past hold point without signoff)
Risk: high (transformers are critical equipment, electrical safety)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: ["non-compliant"] - the sequence violation makes it non-compliant
Percent complete: estimate ~60% (past hold point, in progress)
Confidence: 0.85
Findings: work proceeded past hold point without documented prerequisite signoff
Actions: stop work, obtain signoff for prerequisite step, document hold point release
References: NEC Art. 450, 250.30

    "Work observed past a hold point without the prerequisite step shown or documented signoff page attached",
    "Sequence violation: hold-point release not evidenced before proceeding with transformer installation/termination work",
    "Missing inspection/test documentation for prerequisite step required before energization-related activities under NEC Art. 450",
    "Grounding and bonding per NEC 250.30 cannot be verified as complete without prerequisite signoff"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately at the current hold point",
    "Retrieve and attach the prerequisite step signoff documentation",
    "Have third-party inspector verify and document the prerequisite step before resuming",
    "Confirm transformer grounding/bonding per NEC 250.30 is inspected and signed off before any further work"
  ],
  "rationale": "Work has proceeded past a defined hold point without evidence that the prerequisite step was completed and signed off. In commercial electrical work involving transformers, hold points exist to ensure critical safety items—such as grounding/bonding (NEC 250.30) and transformer installation requirements (NEC Art. 450)—are verified before downstream work continues. Proceeding without documented signoff is a sequence violation and renders the current state non-compliant.",
  "references": ["NEC Art. 450", "NEC 250.30"]
}
